content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if the given number NNN is Armstrong number.

**Note:** Armstrong number is a number that is equal to the sum of its own digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer, NNN - The number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

Print on a single line:

`YES` if NNN is Armstrong number.
`NO` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 10^6).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if N is an Armstrong number, otherwise print "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 0 is considered Armstrong (0^1=0). 1 has 1 digit: 1^1=1 → YES.
9474 is a 4‑digit Armstrong number. 548834 is a 6‑digit Armstrong number.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
YES
YES
NO
YES
YES
YES
YES
YES
YES

solution.approach: For each number N we count its decimal digits d. An Armstrong number satisfies sum_{each digit x of N} x^d = N. We compute d by repeatedly dividing N by 10 (treat N=0 as having one digit). Then we iterate over the digits again, raising each digit to the power d using a simple loop (d ≤ 7, so this is cheap) and accumulating the sum. If the accumulated sum equals N we output YES, otherwise NO. The algorithm is O(d) per test case, where d ≤ 7, and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·d) ≤ O(T·7) i.e. linear in the number of test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides input and output buffers

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty());int N=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());int digits=0;int temp=N;if(temp==0)digits=1;else{while(temp>0){digits++;temp/=10;}}long sum=0;temp=N;if(N==0){sum=0;}else{while(temp>0){int digit=temp%10;long p=1;for(int i=0;i<digits;i++)p*=digit;sum+=p;temp/=10;}}sb.append(sum==N?"YES":"NO");if(t<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
